skill.name: nextjs-15-specialist
skill.description: Guides Next.js 15 App Router work with Server Components, Server Actions, data fetching, caching, metadata, and validation patterns.
skill.summary: Apply modern Next.js 15 App Router patterns with focused guidance and a local pattern validator.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, credential, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are overwhelmingly Markdown examples for Next.js usage. The only executable component is a local Python validator that recursively reads source files from a user-provided directory; this is legitimate for the skill but should be disclosed as a filesystem-read risk.

low_findings.title: Local Validator Recursively Reads Source Files
low_findings.description: The bundled Python validator accepts a directory argument, recursively finds JavaScript and TypeScript files, and reads their contents for pattern checks. This is expected for a validator and does not exfiltrate data, but users should point it only at intended project directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-read behavior is directly implemented and invoked by the CLI entry point. Confidence is not higher because the behavior is local-only, scoped by extension filters, and aligned with the validator purpose.

low_findings.title: Documentation Examples Trigger Network and Secret Patterns
low_findings.description: The Markdown guides include inert examples using fetch calls and environment variables, including an OpenAI API key example. These are instructional snippets, not running marketplace code, so the static network and credential findings are false positives for malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside fenced Markdown TypeScript examples and explain normal Next.js patterns. They contain no executable skill logic and no outbound transmission by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Detections Are Markdown Formatting False Positives
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick detections come from Markdown inline code and fenced examples, such as JSX tags, route filenames, and a documented Python command. I found no shell execution implementation in the skill files reviewed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows documentation text and a user-invoked command example rather than code that executes commands. Additional searches found no subprocess, eval, os.system, or equivalent command execution implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executable Helper Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes validate-patterns.py and recommends running it against a source directory. The script performs local static checks only, with no network calls or shell command execution found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script is explicitly documented and its behavior is visible in source. The risk is limited to local file reads for validation, not confirmed malicious activity.

content.value_statement: Next.js 15 projects often fail when Server Components, caching, or Server Actions are mixed incorrectly. This skill gives practical App Router guidance and a validator for common implementation mistakes.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Server Components or Client Components in Next.js 15.
Provides App Router examples for pages, layouts, route handlers, and dynamic routes.
Guides Server Actions, forms, validation, optimistic updates, and cache revalidation.
Documents fetch caching strategies, revalidation patterns, and request deduplication.
Covers Metadata API patterns for static metadata, dynamic metadata, Open Graph, and JSON-LD.
Includes a Python validator for common Next.js App Router anti-patterns.
limitations: The validator uses regex checks, so it can miss framework issues that need type analysis.
Examples are documentation snippets and may need adaptation for each application architecture.
It does not install dependencies, run tests, or modify a project by itself.
Some references mention a specific Quetrex project context that may not apply to all teams.

use_cases.title: Plan App Router Architecture
use_cases.description: Choose route structure, layouts, loading states, error boundaries, and component boundaries for a Next.js 15 application.

use_cases.title: Review Data Fetching Choices
use_cases.description: Check whether pages use Server Components, fetch cache options, streaming, and revalidation in the right places.

use_cases.title: Audit Server Action Forms
use_cases.description: Evaluate form mutations for validation, authorization, optimistic UI, cache invalidation, and clear error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Component Type
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner guidance for a single component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component requirement and tell me whether it should be a Server Component or Client Component in Next.js 15. Explain the reason and list any needed directives.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Route
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design the App Router files for this feature. Include page, layout, loading, error, route handler, and metadata choices where relevant.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Data Fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Next.js 15 data fetching approach. Identify cache strategy issues, avoidable waterfalls, missing Suspense boundaries, and places to use revalidation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Server Action and related UI for validation, authentication, authorization, cache invalidation, optimistic updates, and error handling in Next.js 15.

output_examples.input: A dashboard page needs user-specific data and a chart with browser interaction.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: keep the page as a Server Component for authenticated data loading, then pass chart data into a small Client Component. Use no-store for user-specific fetches and lazy-load the chart library if it is large.

output_examples.input: A product page needs SEO metadata, cached product data, and hourly updates.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: use generateMetadata for product-specific title and Open Graph fields. Fetch product data with time-based revalidation and reuse the same data function to benefit from request deduplication.

output_examples.input: A create-project form currently posts to an API route and manually refreshes the page.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: use a Server Action with schema validation, return structured field errors, call revalidatePath after mutation, and use form status for pending UI.

best_practices: Default to Server Components until browser APIs, hooks, or direct interactivity are required.
Declare fetch caching behavior explicitly so rendering and freshness are predictable.
Validate Server Action inputs and revalidate affected paths or tags after mutations.
anti_patterns: Adding use client to entire route trees when only one child component needs interactivity.
Calling fetch in Server Components without a clear cache or revalidation strategy.
Trusting Server Action form data without validation, authorization checks, and error handling.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a full Next.js application?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, examples, and validation patterns for building or reviewing Next.js 15 code.

faq.question: Can it help with both Claude and Codex workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does the validator send code to a remote service?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The Python validator reads local files and prints findings locally.

faq.question: What Next.js areas does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers App Router routing, Server Components, Client Components, Server Actions, data fetching, caching, and metadata.

faq.question: Should every fetch use the same cache option?
faq.answer: No. Static, dynamic, personalized, and revalidated data need different cache choices.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with low risk. The main disclosure is that the optional validator reads local project source files.
